summary:
The arterial supply to the submandibular gland comes via a branch originating directly or indirectly from the external carotid artery; among provided choices, option D (Facial) correctly identifies this based common anatomical knowledge despite not being explicitly detailed here.
The primary source feeding blood specifically to the submandibular gland within its territory stems likely as an extension or direct division off what would be considered part of larger networks starting at either maxilla level down towards mandible region including but centered upon facial arteries' role.
